title: How to Think Like Socrates
author: Donald J. Robertson
subline: Ancient philosophy as a way of life in the modern world.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A Greek column (Doric) drawn in hairlines, on a plinth, casting a single red shadow. The column is Socrates' 'pillar' of reason. Title sits below. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1280)" fill="none" stroke-width="2">
    <!-- The plinth (3 stacked rectangles) -->
    <rect x="-180" y="380" width="360" height="20" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <rect x="-200" y="400" width="400" height="20" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <rect x="-220" y="420" width="440" height="30" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <!-- The column shaft (fluted) — 20 vertical hairline grooves -->
    <rect x="-150" y="-100" width="300" height="480" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.7">
      <line x1="-135" y1="-100" x2="-135" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-120" y1="-100" x2="-120" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-105" y1="-100" x2="-105" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-90" y1="-100" x2="-90" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-75" y1="-100" x2="-75" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-60" y1="-100" x2="-60" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-45" y1="-100" x2="-45" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-30" y1="-100" x2="-30" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="-15" y1="-100" x2="-15" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="-100" x2="0" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="15" y1="-100" x2="15" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="30" y1="-100" x2="30" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="45" y1="-100" x2="45" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="-100" x2="60" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="75" y1="-100" x2="75" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="90" y1="-100" x2="90" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="105" y1="-100" x2="105" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="120" y1="-100" x2="120" y2="380"/>
      <line x1="135" y1="-100" x2="135" y2="380"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The capital (Doric) — echinus + abacus -->
    <rect x="-160" y="-140" width="320" height="20" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <rect x="-170" y="-160" width="340" height="20" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <rect x="-180" y="-120" width="360" height="20" stroke="#cccccc"/>
    <!-- A red shadow line on the right side of the column -->
    <line x1="150" y1="-100" x2="150" y2="380" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <!-- A red question mark floating to the left of the column (Socrates' gesture) -->
    <text x="-340" y="-200" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="120" font-weight="300" fill="#fc1c46">?</text>
    <!-- A red question mark floating to the right of the column -->
    <text x="320" y="100" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="120" font-weight="300" fill="#fc1c46">?</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A Greek column IS the 'pillar' of reason Socrates embodied. The cover is a single Doric column drawn in hairlines — plinth, fluted shaft (20 grooves), capital. ONE vertical red shadow line on the right side of the column. Two red question marks float to the left and right — Socrates' interrogative method. Title sits in the bottom 1/3, weight 300 whisper, mixed-case. Brand 'ATHENS' in the top-left; pill '399 BC' at the top — the year of his trial and death.
